prompt:
So the meter-reading push to the billing gateway fails a few times a night and every script that talks to it has grown its own half-baked retry loop. One of them retries 404s forever. Another one sleeps 30 real seconds inside the nightly window. I want ONE helper we can dot-source everywhere and I want it to be testable without actually sleeping, which means the wait itself has to be injectable.

Write `retrydial.ps1` defining a single function `Invoke-ResilientRequest` with parameters `-Uri` (string), `-MaxAttempts` (int), `-BaseDelaySeconds` (double), and `-SleepHook` (scriptblock, defaulting to a real `Start-Sleep`). It GETs the URI and:

- 2xx: done. Return an object with properties `Status` (int), `Attempts` (how many requests it took), and `Body` (the raw response body string, untouched).
- 5xx or 429: transient. If attempts remain, wait and try again. The wait for the k-th retry is `BaseDelaySeconds * 2^(k-1)` (so base, doubled each time) — UNLESS the response that made us retry carried a `Retry-After` header (delta-seconds, possibly fractional), in which case THAT value is the wait, full stop. Waiting happens by invoking the sleep hook with the number of seconds as its argument — never call Start-Sleep directly in the retry path, or the tests (which pass a recording hook and pin the exact schedule) will crawl and fail.
- Any other 4xx: permanent, do NOT retry, throw `retrydial: client error <status>, not retrying`. A 404 is a wrong URL, not a flaky gateway — hitting it again is how we got throttled in the first place.
- Out of attempts: throw `retrydial: gave up after <MaxAttempts> attempts (last status <status>)`.

Exact message texts and the exact recorded sleep schedules are pinned by `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_retrydial.ps1`, which spins up a local mock gateway and also checks the request counts the gateway saw per path (fail-fast paths must see exactly one request). Leave the test file and mock_flaky.py alone.
notes: 6P6 item 2: retrying client with status-class taxonomy. Python stdlib mock scripts per-path status sequences (500/503+Retry-After/200, endless 500, 429 with and without Retry-After, fail-fast 400/404); harness pins the driver block byte-exact (recorded sleep schedules incl. Retry-After override and exponential doubling), pins the gateway-side request log (fail-fast = exactly one request, gave-up = exactly MaxAttempts), and a generous 12s wall guard trips implementations that really sleep the ~19s nominal schedule instead of calling the hook. Sleep hook is a scriptblock param per the catalog. Retry-After is delta-seconds only (HTTP-date needs wall-clock 'now' — banned by wave-6 determinism law). Distinct from go-httpretry/py-retry-policy/ts-retry-abort (policy/backoff engines in-process) by the live-loopback taxonomy + server-side count pinning in pwsh. catalog wave6/6P6/2.
